name: mosaic-mem
description: Combine explicit 3D patch tracking with implicit model-based memory for video world models. Agents generate minutes-long consistent video with camera motion, object persistence, and editable scene elements. Enable embodied agents to imagine and plan with realistic video rollouts.
paperTitle: MosaicMem - Hybrid Spatial Memory for Controllable Video World Models
keywords: world models, video generation, spatial memory, 3D consistency, video diffusion
content:
# MosaicMem: Hybrid Spatial Memory for Video World Models

Video diffusion models excel at short clips but struggle with long-horizon consistency: camera motion creates drift, objects disappear/reappear. World models need to remember what persists. MosaicMem uses hybrid memory: explicit 3D patches for reliable localization + implicit model conditioning for realistic generation.

## Why This Matters for Agents

Agents plan by imagining consequences. Video world models enable this, but existing models can't maintain consistency over minutes. MosaicMem enables 1-2 minute video rollouts with stable camera motion and object persistence—agents can realistically imagine long-horizon futures.

## Core Concept

Two memory types: (1) Explicit: 3D patch coordinates and pixel values (reliable but limited), (2) Implicit: diffusion model conditioning (flexible but may drift). Hybrid approach: use explicit for localization, implicit for photorealism. Compose patches in queried view via patch-and-compose interface.

## Implementation

```python
class MosaicMemModule(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, video_model, patch_size=64):
        super().__init__()
        self.video_model = video_model
        self.patch_size = patch_size
        self.patch_memory = {}  # {time_t: {(x,y): patch_pixels}}
        self.camera_trajectory = []
    
    def add_frame(self, frame, camera_pose, timestamp):
        """Add frame to spatial memory."""
        # Extract 3D patches from frame
        patches = self._extract_3d_patches(frame, camera_pose)
        
        # Store with pose (enables reprojection)
        self.patch_memory[timestamp] = patches
        self.camera_trajectory.append(camera_pose)
    
    def _extract_3d_patches(self, frame, camera_pose):
        """Extract 3D-localized patches."""
        patches = {}
        for i in range(0, frame.shape[1], self.patch_size):
            for j in range(0, frame.shape[2], self.patch_size):
                patch = frame[:, i:i+self.patch_size, j:j+self.patch_size]
                # Lift patch to 3D using monocular depth
                depth = self.estimate_depth(patch)
                world_pos = self.camera_to_world(camera_pose, i, j, depth)
                patches[world_pos] = patch
        return patches
    
    def generate_future(self, current_frame, camera_motion, num_steps=60):
        """Generate future frames with consistent memory."""
        generated_frames = [current_frame]
        current_camera = self.camera_trajectory[-1]
        
        for step in range(num_steps):
            # Project patches to current viewpoint
            next_camera = current_camera + camera_motion
            projected_patches = self._reproject_patches(next_camera)
            
            # Compose patches in current view
            composed_image = self._compose_patches(projected_patches, current_frame.shape)
            
            # Diffusion refinement (inpaint gaps)
            refined = self.video_model.inpaint(
                composed_image,
                condition={"camera": next_camera},
                steps=10
            )
            
            generated_frames.append(refined)
            current_camera = next_camera
        
        return generated_frames
    
    def _reproject_patches(self, new_camera):
        """Reproject 3D patches to new camera viewpoint."""
        reprojected = {}
        for timestamp, patches in self.patch_memory.items():
            for world_pos, patch in patches.items():
                # Transform world_pos to new camera frame
                view_pos = self.world_to_camera(new_camera, world_pos)
                pixel_pos = self.camera_to_pixel(view_pos)
                reprojected[pixel_pos] = patch
        return reprojected
```

## When to Use

- Long-horizon video world models (planning)
- Video editing/scene manipulation
- Embodied agent imagination

## Reference

- **Paper**: arXiv:2603.17117 (March 2026). MosaicMem by multi-institutional team.
- **Capability**: 1-2 minute consistent video generation with editable scene elements
- **Methods**: PRoPE camera conditioning, memory alignment, patch composition
